pi_items.source: Task description and setup_task.sh configuration scripts
Task description and setup_task.sh configuration scripts
Task description and Unsteady flow simulation execution in setup_task.sh

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the standard 'Muncie' HEC-RAS unsteady flow example model, representing the White River in Indiana. By independently executing the unsteady flow simulation and extracting data from 'Muncie.p04.hdf', it is confirmed that the most upstream cross-section corresponds to River Station 15696.24 (index 0). The peak flow at this upstream boundary occurs precisely at simulation time index 180, reaching a flow rate of 21,000 cfs. Analyzing the Water Surface Elevation (WSE) and Velocity fields across all cross-sections at time index 180 reveals exactly 0 Energy Grade Line (EGL) consistency violations (i.e., no downstream node exhibits an EGL higher than its immediate upstream neighbor plus 0.01 tolerance). Additionally, a severe defect was discovered in the task's ground truth generation logic ('export_result.sh'). The script attempts to access HDF5 cross-section attributes using the key 'River Station', but HEC-RAS 6.6 geometry files utilize the key 'RS'. This KeyError is silently caught, resulting in an empty ground truth profile. Consequently, the verifier will always award an agent 0 out of 40 points for the 'Data values match ground truth snapshot' criteria, unfairly capping the maximum achievable task score at 60 points regardless of agent performance.
